checks.a_analysis: The output follows the source document's structure for the sections it includes. It does not contain hallucinated headings, invented body text, or made-up conclusions. The only additions are acceptable metadata and structural placeholders.

I verified every phone number, address, and statutory reference in the Appendix A tables against the source document. The output correctly reflects the phone numbers (e.g., 828/697-4576, 919/716-3501), addresses (e.g., 200 N. Grove Street, Hendersonville), and General Statutes mentioned in the Executive Summary (§143-725). No fabricated contact information was found.

The output document suffers from a severe truncation. It completely omits pages 4 through 30 of the source document. This missing range includes the second half of the Executive Summary, the entirety of Section I (Purpose), Section II (Detailed Accomplishments), Section III (Action Plan), and Appendix B (Establishing Authority). The JSON explicitly includes a placeholder indicating that 28 sections were omitted.
checks.b_evidence_source: Entire content from Page 4 (Executive Summary cont.) through Page 30 (Appendices list/Appendix B).

checks.a_analysis: I counted the rows in the tables provided in Appendix A and Appendix C. The tables in the output correspond row-for-row with the tables on pages 31-34 and 36-38 of the source PDF. No data rows appear to be missing from the tables that were actually processed.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The Executive Summary section is severely truncated. While the bulleted accomplishments from page 3 are present, the detailed narrative paragraphs summarizing key initiatives (NC OneMap, Orthoimagery, Seamless Parcels, Address NC, Street Centerlines, Census Collaboration, etc.) that appear on page 4 are entirely missing. This represents a loss of approximately 40% of the Executive Summary section's total content.
checks.b_evidence_source: Executive Summary text on page 4, starting with 'NC OneMap (http://data.nconemap.com), a priority initiative...' and ending with '...mutual benefits (Section III).'
checks.b_evidence_output: The Executive Summary ends at element index 26 with content from page 3.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: For the content that was converted, the text faithfully preserves the meaning and wording of the source. Minor reformatting such as the collapse of multiline table cells into a single string is acceptable.

checks.a_analysis: The document structure follows the page-by-page content of the source PDF. Major sections and tables (Appendices A and C) are divided by page as they appear in the original, without repeating text blocks from previous sections. No duplication was found in the headings or lists provided in the JSON snippet.

The output maintains a logical heading hierarchy. Major sections like 'Executive Summary' and the Appendices are at H2, while subsections (like the specific categories in the summary and the FY breakdown in Appendix C) are at H3. While the title page level hierarchy is slightly flat (multiple H1s for the title), it correctly follows the logical order and does not skip levels (e.g., H1 to H3).

There are three structural defects in the tables. 1. In Appendix A (Section 34), the row containing 'Advisory Members' includes only one cell with 'colspan: 1', creating an inconsistent column count for a 3-column table. 2. In Appendix C (Section 37), the first row of data ('NCDOT Pavement Condition Survey...') is misidentified as a header row, whereas the source PDF does not repeat headers on this page. 3. In Appendix C (Section 38), the first row ('Shellfish Growing Areas') is similarly misidentified as a header row.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 34: 'Advisory Members' is a spanning section row. Page 37: Table starts with 'NCDOT Pavement Condition Survey...'. Page 38: Table starts with 'Shellfish Growing Areas'.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 34, element 281: row with 1 cell ('Advisory Members'). Section 37, element 296: row 0 'is_header_row': true. Section 38, element 298: row 0 'is_header_row': true.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order aligns with the logical top-to-bottom, left-to-right flow of the source document. The title page, executive summary, and appendices are ordered correctly according to the PDF's visual layout.

The lists in the Executive Summary correctly capture the items denoted by checkmarks in the source. They are rendered as unordered lists, which preserves the meaning and relationship of the items to their subheadings.
Links identified in the source (e.g., in Figure 6 and the Appendix headers) are correctly preserved in the output text. No fabricated or broken links were detected in the reviewed sections.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the single-column visual organization of the source PDF. The sequence of cover page, executive summary, table of contents, and numbered sections follows the original document's intent. While margins and font styles differ, the visual hierarchy of headings and the placement of images relative to text blocks are consistent with the source.

Tables found in Appendix A (membership list, pages 31-34) and Appendix C (data updates, pages 36-38) are correctly rendered in the HTML. The columns for Member names, Title/Organization, and Appointing Authority are properly aligned with distinct headers. The data is clear and legible, retaining the tabular structure from the PDF.

All meaningful visual elements from the source PDF are present in the HTML rendering. This includes the organizational logo (p. 1), Figure 1 (p. 7), Figure 2 (p. 8), various orthoimagery examples (Figures 3, 5, and photos on pages 6, 9, 16, 17), the acquisition cycle map (Figure 4, p. 13), the portal screenshot (Figure 6, p. 15), the parcels project map (Figure 7, p. 19), and the conference banner (p. 24). There are no broken image placeholders.

A review of the rendered HTML shows no instances of unintended content duplication. While the 'Executive Summary' lists (NC OneMap, Orthoimagery, etc.) appear twice (once at the start and once at the beginning of Section II), this duplication exists in the source PDF on pages 3 and 10 respectively and is therefore a faithful conversion of the original content. The Table of Contents navigation at the top is standard accessible design and not a duplication defect.
